problem.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal II
problem.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the bottom-up level order traversal of its nodes' values. (i.e., from left to right, level by level from leaf to root).

example.explanation: Bottom-up level order: deepest level first. Reverse of standard level order.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – BFS with Reversal
solutions.description: Perform a standard BFS level-order traversal using a queue, collecting each level's values into a list. Each new level is prepended (inserted at index 0) to the result list so that the final order is bottom-up without an explicit reversal step at the end. The key insight is that inserting at the front during traversal is equivalent to reversing afterward but avoids an extra O(n) pass.
